title: A for loop update clause should move the counter in the right direction
description: A "for" loop update clause should move the counter in the right directionAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoIn aforloop, the update clause is responsible for modifying the loop counter variable in the appropriate direction to control the loop’s iteration. It determines how the loop counter variable changes with each iteration of the loop. The loop counter should move in the right direction to prevent infinite loops or unexpected behavior.This rule raises an issue when the loop counter is updated in the wrong direction with respect to the loop termination condition.for (let i = 0; i < strings.length; i--) { // Noncompliant: The counter 'i' is decremented, making the loop infinite //..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: For loop counter moves in wrong direction. When condition uses '<' or '<=', use increment (++). When condition uses '>' or '>=', use decrement (--) to avoid infinite loops.
